# VeilChat — Keyword Map

A focused, hand-curated set of ~220 high-intent search phrases for the
private-messenger niche, grouped by **search intent** and mapped to the
**existing blog posts** that target each cluster. No auto-generated
filler — every phrase is one real people actually search for.

> **A note on volume estimates.** Real monthly-search-volume numbers
> require a paid tool (Ahrefs, Semrush, Mangools). Where it matters,
> phrases are tagged **HIGH / MED / LOW** based on category-typical
> demand from public sources (Google Suggest, related searches,
> AnswerThePublic, Reddit). Treat these as rough ordering, not exact
> traffic.

---

## Intent legend

| Tag | What the searcher wants | SEO leverage |
|---|---|---|
| **INFO** | To learn what something is. Top-of-funnel. | Build authority, capture long-tail. |
| **CMP** | To compare 2+ products. Mid-funnel. | Highest conversion intent on the web. |
| **ALT** | To replace something they already use. Mid-funnel. | Excellent conversion intent. |
| **HOW** | A specific task. Mid/bottom-funnel. | Long-tail, low competition, sticky. |
| **THM** | Looking for a tool for a specific role/threat. Bottom. | Small volume, very high quality. |
| **BRD** | Searching for you by name. Bottom. | Defensive — must rank #1 yourself. |

---

## 2. COMPARISON — "X vs Y"

## What to actually do with this list

1. **Don't put these in a `<meta name="keywords">` tag.** Google
   ignores it and competitors will laugh at you.
2. **Write naturally.** Each keyword is a topic, not a string to be
   stuffed. If your `/blog/signal-vs-whatsapp` post discusses the
   things people search for in cluster 2, it will rank for many
   variations of the same question.
3. **Use this list as a content roadmap.** When you (or a writer)
   publish a new post, start by asking "which 5–10 keywords from this
   map does this cover?" If the answer is "none", reconsider.
4. **Check your existing rankings monthly** in Google Search Console
   → Performance → Queries. The keywords you're already showing for
   (even on page 5) are your easiest wins — improve those posts
   first.
5. **The 10–20 highest-leverage missing topics** based on this map:
   - "WhatsApp alternatives in Europe" (high commercial intent post-DMA)
   - "Best encrypted messenger for Android" (huge volume)
   - "Best encrypted messenger for iPhone" (huge volume)
   - "Telegram alternatives" (under-served by your existing posts)
   - "How to leave WhatsApp" (high intent, easy to win)
   - "How to switch from WhatsApp to Signal" (very high intent)
   - "Is WhatsApp safe?" (your biggest single keyword opportunity)
   - "Pegasus spyware explained" (newsworthy, sustained interest)
   - "WhatsApp ads — what's coming" (timely, high CTR)
   - "Self-hosted messenger comparison" (small but high-quality audience)
